title: Chamaeraphis schiedeana
taxonomy: Chamaeraphis schiedeana is classified within the family Asparagaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Chamaeraphis schiedeana is further classified within the subfamily Agavoideae. The subfamily Agavoideae is a group of plants that are commonly known as agaves and allies. intro: Chamaeraphis schiedeana, commonly known as Mexican chamomile or Guanajuato chamomile, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae that is native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, promote relaxation, and aid in the treatment of insomnia. Chamaeraphis schiedeana contains bioactive constituents such as apigenin, luteolin, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Chamaeraphis schiedeana is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to promote sleep, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression.
